resposta:
solution: 
```python
class Solution:
    def maxFreq(self, s: str, maxLetters: int, minSize: int, maxSize: int) -> int:
        from collections import defaultdict
        n = len(s)
        max_occ = 0
        freq = defaultdict(int)
        
        # Only need to check minSize because any longer valid substring
        # will have <= occurrences of its minSize prefix
        size = minSize
        for i in range(n - size + 1):
            sub = s[i:i+size]
            unique = len(set(sub))
            if unique <= maxLetters:
                freq[sub] += 1
                if freq[sub] > max_occ:
                    max_occ = freq[sub]
        return max_occ
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n * minSize)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Sliding-window counting of substrings of exactly minSize; any longer valid substring cannot appear more often than its minSize prefix, so we can ignore maxSize.
